frictions.description: Live rerun was initially blocked by environment drift: earlier evidence targeted stale fixtures, the active listeners moved between `3000/1999` and `3100/2999`, and PartyKit websocket auth only succeeded once it was restarted with explicit runtime vars instead of relying on wrapper-script env propagation.
frictions.resolution: Seeded a fresh isolated fixture user/workflow, restarted a coherent local stack (`web:3100`, `broadcast:1999`) with `LOCALHOST_PUBLIC_DOMAIN`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_BROADCAST_URL`, and `INTERNAL_SHARED_SECRET` aligned, then captured a real terminal daemon-event ack plus user-channel websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` delivery.

appliedUpdates.description: Added non-production test-context daemon auth seam gated by internal shared secret and explicit test headers; production auth remains unchanged.
Added test coverage for test-auth acceptance/rejection and terminal flow broadcast with refetch:["delivery-loop"].
Upgraded VAL-CROSS-001 report with live end-to-end evidence: daemon-event ack + websocket delivery-loop refetch capture.

flowReports.summary: Daemon terminal events trigger broadcast with delivery-loop refetch wiring in daemon-event route.
No-spam behavior remains protected by material-change checks and route dedup/idempotency tests.

Authentication blocker is removed in test context and proven live: a freshly seeded terminal v2 daemon-event under test-auth acknowledged on `web:3100` and emitted websocket `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` on `broadcast:1999` for the fresh thread/chat.
evidence: pnpm -C apps/www exec vitest run src/app/api/daemon-event/route.test.ts
